evalDetails.reasoning: The model produced a near-perfect async/await conversion. All four functions are properly converted to async with no callbacks remaining. The error messages are preserved verbatim with identical prefixes. The sequential operation order (readFile → parseData → validateRecords → transformRecords → writeFile) is fully preserved with proper sequential awaits. The 'no valid records' guard remains at the exact same point in the pipeline. The validateRecords return value is elegantly adapted from two callback args to a single object `{validRecords, invalidCount}` — a necessary and clean adaptation. No .then()/.catch() chains are used. The removal of setTimeout delays from helper functions is correct since those were only simulating async behavior, and async functions already return promises. The only minor gap is the parallel operations criterion (4/5): the pipeline is inherently sequential so Promise.all doesn't apply, but the model didn't explicitly acknowledge this. Code quality is clean and readable with no unnecessary nesting.
